source.name: CA State Water Board — Freshwater & Estuarine HAB (FHAB) Program

blooms.name: American River
blooms.detail: Updates to this report are provided by a routine water monitoring program conducting site visits at this waterway, refer to Organization field above for more information.

blooms.name: American River
blooms.detail: Water testing of samples resulted in no exceedance of an advisory trigger threshold.
blooms.size: between a tennis court and a sedan
blooms.landmark: 700 feet  downstream of Watt Ave Bridge near small island

blooms.name: North Natomas Retention Basin
blooms.detail: Water testing of samples indicated no harmful algal bloom produced by cyanobacteria was present. Other types of nuisance algae are present that are not known to produce toxins.
blooms.landmark: Heritage Park near Elkhorn Blvd. and Natomas Blvd.

blooms.name: Mather Lake
blooms.detail: Suspected illness was reported and is under investigation by the Interagency HAB-related Illness Workgroup, no determination has been made.

blooms.name: McKinley Park
blooms.detail: Visual observation of harmful algal bloom from shoreline or boat. Water testing was not conducted to measure toxicity of bloom according to statewide guidelines. Contact the water or land manager listed above to request water testing due to the confirmed visible bloom.
blooms.texture: Floating mats; Green discoloration; Spilled paint

blooms.name: Pleasant Grove Creek South Fork
blooms.detail: Observations at this site include confirmation of harmful algal bloom that  forms potential toxic algal mats that grow attached to cobble, sand, and plants on the bottom of the waterway. The water can appear clear when algal mats are attached to the bottom. These mats can dislodge and float downstream or strand on the shoreline, children and dogs should avoid touching mat material.

blooms.name: Lower American River
blooms.detail: Visual observations of suspicious algae reported - Water Boards staff determined that no harmful algal bloom produced by cyanobacteria was present. Other types of nuisance algae were present.

blooms.name: Pocket Canal
blooms.detail: Observations at this site include confirmation of harmful algal bloom that forms floating/planktonic blooms. Children and dogs should avoid skin contact with bloom material that may appear as discolored water, milky water, with a surface sheen (like spilled paint), or scum.
blooms.size: between a football field and a tennis court

blooms.name: Pocket Canal

blooms.name: Camden Lake
blooms.detail: Advisory applies to the full extent of the lake or reservoir (lake-wide advisory) and is not to limited the location symbolized by this report.  Alert! High concentration of one or more cyanotoxins measured at this site indicating elevated health risk to humans and animals.
